HEADS OF TERMS FOR THE PROPOSED ACQUISITION OF [NAME OF BUSINESS] (the
“Company”)
SUBJECT TO LEGAL CONTRACT AND DUE DILIGENCE
PARTIES:
1. Shareholder 1, name and address
2. Shareholder 2, name and address (continue adding as necessary)
(together “the Vendors”)
4. Your name and address
(”Purchaser”)
These Heads of Terms have been prepared to give guidance to the lawyers of the parties for the preparation of
the legal documentation in relation to the proposed sale of [NAME OF BUSINESS] (or “the Company”) (the
“Transaction”).
These Heads of Terms are not exhaustive and are not intended to be legally binding between the parties except
as specifically set out in these Heads of Terms.
TERMS:
1. 	Principal Financial Terms
1.1 	The Purchaser (or a suitable vehicle set up on the Purchaser’s behalf (“Newco”)) is to acquire from the
Vendors X% of the entire issued share capital of [NAME OF BUSINESS], for a cash consideration of
$X,XXX,XXX.
1.2 	$XXX,XXX of the consideration is to be paid at completion of the Transaction (“Completion”) and the
remaining $X,XXX,XXX is to be paid annually over X years (i.e. $XXX,XXX per year). The deferred
consideration payments are not contingent on future financial performance.
[If your deal has contingencies applied to the deferred consideration, list here.]
1.3 	The Purchaser or Newco will enter into a X year option agreement with the Vendors, allowing the
acquisition of the remaining Y% shareholding in [NAME OF BUSINESS], for a consideration of $Z. [Note: If
your deal has this]
Heads of Terms/LOI Template
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 4 --

1.4 	The consideration to be paid for the initial purchase of X% of the issued shares, is based on an
anticipated balance sheet (measured as net current assets) at Completion of $A. It is also anticipated that the
retained cash balance at completion is $B. Therefore final consideration is to be adjusted upwards or downwards
on a $ for $ basis, should the balance sheet and/or cash exceed or fall short of $A. [Note: can also do this for
working capital, cash, or a combination of all these metrics]
1.5 	The Purchaser or Newco will also assume the following liabilities at completion,which are also subject to
the completion balance sheet, working capital and cash requirements in reference to 1.4.
Item 1: Short term cash flow debt 	$XXX,XXX
Item 2: Long term cash flow debt 	$XXX,XXX
Item 3: Trade payables 	$XXX,XXX
Item 4: Taxes due 	$XXX,XXX
Total: 	$X,XXX,XXX
2. 	Other matters
2.1 	X is to continue as a Director of the Company until the Purchaser has exercised the option on the
remaining X% shareholding still owned by the Vendors or until such time as either of them wishes to retire from
the Board.
2.2 	X is to continue working in the business for a salary of $Y per annum until the Purchaser has taken
advantage of the option to purchase the remaining X% shareholding owned by the Vendors.
2.3 	[The Vendors, the Purchaser (or Newco as applicable) and [NAME OF BUSINESS] will enter into a
shareholders agreement to govern the relationship between the parties and how the Company will be run after
Completion.]
3. 	Consents and Due Diligence
3.1 	The Transaction is still conditional upon the Purchaser being satisfied with the results of their due
diligence exercise.
4. 	Warranties and Indemnities
4.1 	The sale will be documented through a purchase and sale agreement. This agreement will include
warranties and indemnities customary for a transaction of this kind.
5. 	Information
5.1 	Subject always to clause 7 and until completion the Vendors will allow the Purchaser and his agents
access to such information as is reasonably necessary to further evaluate the proposed acquisition and to assist
as far as is possible in any due diligence exercises.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 4 --

6. 	Exclusivity
6.1 	Up to and including 1st January 2023, or until the Purchaser notifies the Vendors that he no longer
wishes to proceed with the negotiations contemplated by this document, if earlier (the “Exclusivity Period”), the
Vendors will not:
1. 	Directly or indirectly solicit or initiate any approach by any person other than the Purchaser
with a view to an acquisition of their shares in the Company;
2. 	Enter into any agreement or arrangement with anyone other than the Purchaser relating to an
acquisition of their shares in the Company.
6.2 	If the Vendors are in breach of Clause 6.1 above, then they undertake to coverall costs reasonably
incurred by the Purchaser in respect of the Transaction from the date of these Heads of Terms, subject to a
maximum amount of $XX,XXX (including disbursements, but excluding applicable sales taxes).
The Purchaser undertakes to inform the Vendors immediately in writing if at any stage during the due diligence
process he has good reason to believe that the acquisition of the Company cannot proceed in accordance with
these Heads of Terms.
The Purchaser intends to proceed as quickly as possible with the proposed purchase and will negotiate in good
faith with a view to completing the Transaction on or before 1st January 2023.
7. 	Confidentiality
7.1 	No announcement or other disclosure will be made concerning the contents of this document, the
proposed acquisition or any ancillary matter except as required by any applicable law or any regulatory authority
or with the written approval of the Vendors and The Purchaser.
7.2 	The provision of information by the Vendors or the Company to the Purchaser or his advisors in relation
to the Company, will be treated confidentially.
7.3 	This paragraph 7 is legally binding.
8. 	Timing and Survival of Terms
8.1 	The parties will work towards execution of legally binding documentation for the proposed acquisition
by 1st January 2023.
8.2 	If this is not achieved by 1st January 2023, then the agreement in principle represented by this
document will terminate on that date, but the provisions of paragraphs 7 and 9 and this paragraph 8 will remain
legally binding on all parties to the agreement.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 4 --

8.3 	The parties are responsible for their own costs in connection with the Transaction, whether or not it
proceeds (including (without limitation)) the preparation and negotiation of these Heads of Terms) and any
party may end negotiations in relation to the proposed Transaction without having to give reason for doing so.
9. 	Confirmation and Acceptance
1. 	This agreement and the negotiations between the Purchaser and the Vendors concerning the
proposed acquisition of the Company shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the law of [list US
state as relevant] and each party agrees to submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Courts of [list US state as
relevant] as regards any claim or matter arising under or in connection with this document.
2. 	Except as specifically set out in paragraphs 6, 7, 8 and 9 above, this document is not intended to
be legally binding and does not constitute an offer capable of becoming a contract by acceptance.
3. 	These Heads of Terms are for the benefit of the parties to them and are not intended to benefit,
or be enforceable, by anyone else.
